problem_title: Audit trails weaken across adverse media reviews
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to preserve clear evidence for adverse media review decisions when screening outputs and case management remain disconnected. This gap slows review workflows and undermines defensible audit records needed for regulatory and internal scrutiny.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links screening results, reviewer actions, case records and decision evidence into one workflow that preserves audit-ready trails for adverse media compliance teams in digital asset businesses.
workflow_title: From adverse media alerts to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes adverse media alerts through structured review, case creation, decision logging and evidence preservation to support audit-ready compliance outcomes.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects adverse media screening outputs into structured reviews, case workflows, decision logs and preserved evidence for clearer, audit-ready compliance control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for adverse media compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance layer that connects adverse media screening outputs into structured reviews, cases, decisions and evidence for digital asset businesses and VASPs.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle adverse media cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY organizes adverse media alerts into cases, routes them for review, logs decisions and collects evidence, enabling consistent workflows and traceability across compliance teams.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and supporting evidence with audit-ready records to support regulatory reviews and internal compliance traceability.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing screening providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with multiple adverse media and sanctions screening providers, consolidating their outputs into a connected compliance workflow without disrupting existing tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace adverse media screening tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace screening tools but connects their results into structured workflows, decision records and evidence packages for review and reporting.
final_title: Preserve adverse media evidence with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY structures adverse media outputs into audit-ready cases, decisions and evidence for clearer compliance workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
